docs.title: OneManCompany Documentation
docs.text: Welcome to the OneManCompany documentation. OneManCompany is an open-source AI operating system that lets anyone build and run a complete AI-powered company from their browser.

docs.title: What is OneManCompany?
docs.text:
You are the CEO — the only human. Everyone else — HR, COO, engineers, designers — are AI employees that think, collaborate, and deliver real work autonomously.

  • Not a chatbot — a company with org structure, hiring, task management, performance reviews, and knowledge management
  • Not a demo — delivers production-grade output (games, comics, apps)
  • Not a framework — a complete platform you can run from your browser

docs.title: 1-on-1 Coaching
docs.text: One of the most powerful features in OneManCompany: CEO guidance sessions that permanently shape how an employee works.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
When you coach an employee through a 1-on-1 session:

  1. You select an employee and start a guidance session
  2. You provide feedback, direction, or new principles
  3. The system distills your guidance into the employee's work principles (work_principles.md)
  4. From that point forward, the employee's behavior reflects your coaching

This isn't a temporary instruction — it's a permanent behavioral change, just like how a real mentor shapes a junior employee over time.

docs.title: What You Can Coach On
docs.text:
• Work style — "Be more concise in your reports", "Always include test cases"
  • Technical approach — "Prefer functional patterns over OOP", "Use TypeScript strictly"
  • Communication — "Summarize decisions at the end of every meeting"
  • Quality standards — "Never ship without unit tests", "Always consider edge cases"
  • Domain knowledge — "Our users are non-technical, keep UX simple"

docs.title: How It Persists
docs.text:
Your guidance is written into the employee's work_principles.md file:

employees/
└── 00006/
    ├── profile.yaml
    ├── work_principles.md    ← Your coaching lives here
    └── guidance.yaml

Every time the employee works on a task, their work principles are injected into their reasoning context. The employee literally thinks differently after your coaching.

docs.title: Tips for Effective Coaching
docs.text:
Be Specific

Instead of "write better code", say "always add error handling for network calls and log the error details before retrying."

Coach After Reviewing Work

The best time to coach is after reviewing a task deliverable. Concrete feedback tied to real output is more impactful than abstract principles.

Cumulative Effect

Each coaching session builds on previous ones. Over time, your employees develop a sophisticated understanding of your standards and preferences.

docs.title: Organizational Evolution
docs.text:
Coaching isn't just about individual improvement. The cumulative effect across your team creates organizational evolution:

  • Employee-level insights become work principles
  • Project-level lessons become company knowledge base
  • Combined, your AI company gets better with every project

This is why OneManCompany is an operating system, not just a tool — the organization itself learns and improves.

docs.title: Company Configuration
docs.text: Three configuration layers let you shape your AI company's identity and behavior — making the same OS run completely different companies.

docs.title: Company Direction
docs.text:
Your vision statement, injected into every employee's reasoning. Change the direction, and the entire company pivots.

What it controls:

  • What products to build
  • Which market to target
  • Strategic priorities
  • Quality vs speed trade-offs

Example:

"We are a mobile-first game studio targeting casual gamers aged 18-35. We prioritize polished UX and rapid iteration over feature completeness."

Every employee — from COO breaking down tasks to engineers writing code — factors this direction into their decisions.

docs.title: Company Culture
docs.text:
Behavioral principles that govern how every employee works. Same Talents, same Vessels, completely different company personality.

What it controls:

  • Communication style (formal vs casual)
  • Decision-making approach (move fast vs be thorough)
  • Collaboration norms
  • Quality standards

Example:

  • Ship fast, iterate faster. MVP over perfection.
  • Write code that a junior dev can read.
  • Every PR must include tests. No exceptions.
  • Default to async communication. Meetings only when blocking.

docs.title: Workflows
docs.text:
Workflow definitions in company_rules/ control how the company operates at a process level.

What they define:

  • How tasks flow through the organization
  • Approval chains and quality gates
  • Standard operating procedures
  • Automation triggers

Workflows are defined as Markdown files, parsed by the workflow engine, and dispatched to the appropriate handlers.

docs.title: Configuration Files
docs.text: File What It Controls Company Direction docs Strategic vision and priorities Company Culture docs Behavioral norms and standards company_rules/*.md Workflow definitions and SOPs employees/{id}/profile.yaml Individual employee configuration employees/{id}/guidance.yaml Employee-specific guidance notes

docs.title: The OS Analogy
docs.text:
This is what makes OneManCompany an operating system:

  • Same iOS can be a work phone or a kid's gaming device — depends on apps and settings
  • Same OneManCompany can be a game studio or a dev agency — depends on Direction, Culture, and Talents

Swap the configuration, swap the company.

docs.title: Cost Tracking
docs.text: OneManCompany tracks LLM token usage and USD cost per project, so you always know what your AI company is spending.

docs.title: What's Tracked
docs.text:
• Token usage — Input and output tokens per employee per task
  • USD cost — Calculated from model pricing (varies by provider and model)
  • Per-project breakdown — See exactly how much each project costs

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
Every LLM call made by an employee is logged with:

  • Employee ID
  • Task/project context
  • Model used
  • Token count (prompt + completion)
  • Estimated cost

docs.title: Viewing Costs
docs.text:
Cost data is available in the CEO console. You can see:

  • Per-task costs — How much each individual task consumed
  • Per-project costs — Total cost across all subtasks in a project
  • Per-employee costs — Which employees are most expensive

docs.title: Cost Optimization Tips
docs.text:
Choose Models Wisely

Each employee can be assigned a different model. Use cheaper models for simple tasks (email, summaries) and more capable models for complex work (coding, design).

Use Claude Code for Heavy Work

If you have a Claude subscription, switching founding employees to Claude Code mode for complex tasks can reduce per-token OpenRouter costs.

Monitor Project Costs Early

Check costs during a project, not just after. If a task is burning through tokens, consider providing clearer instructions or breaking it into smaller pieces.

docs.title: Cost vs Quality
docs.text:
Different models offer different cost/quality trade-offs:

Tier Example Models Best For Budget Smaller open models Simple tasks, high volume Mid-range Claude Haiku, GPT-4o-mini Routine work, coordination Premium Claude Opus, GPT-4o Complex reasoning, coding

The flexibility to assign different models per employee lets you optimize this balance across your organization.

docs.title: Execution Modes
docs.text: Founding employees (EA, HR, COO, CSO) support two execution modes. You can switch between them in the browser settings panel.

docs.title: Company Hosted Agent
docs.text:
The default mode. OneManCompany's built-in agent framework handles everything.

  • How it works: Tasks are executed by OMC's internal LangChain-based agent, calling LLMs through OpenRouter
  • Requirements: OpenRouter API Key (configured during setup)
  • Best for: Getting started quickly, lower cost, full control over model selection
  • Model flexibility: Each employee can be assigned a different model in their profile

docs.title: Claude Code
docs.text:
Employees run as Claude Code CLI sessions, leveraging Anthropic's most capable coding agent.

  • How it works: Each task spawns a Claude Code CLI session with MCP tools connecting back to the company
  • Requirements: Claude Pro or Max subscription with Claude Code CLI installed
  • Best for: Complex coding tasks, stronger autonomous reasoning, access to Claude's full tool ecosystem

Subscription Required

Claude Code mode requires an active Claude Pro ($20/mo) or Max ($100/mo) subscription. The subscription is managed through your Anthropic account, separate from OpenRouter.

docs.title: Switching Modes
docs.text:
1. Open the Settings panel in the browser
  2. Select the employee you want to configure
  3. Choose between Company Hosted and Claude Code
  4. The change takes effect on the next task

docs.title: How They Compare
docs.text: Company Hosted Agent Claude Code Cost model Pay-per-token via OpenRouter Flat subscription fee Setup Just an API key Claude CLI + subscription Model choice Any model on OpenRouter Claude (Anthropic) Coding ability Good (depends on model) Excellent Tool access OMC built-in tools Full Claude Code + MCP tools Best for General tasks, budget control Complex dev work

docs.title: Hybrid Setup
docs.text:
You can mix modes across employees. For example:

  • EA, HR, CSO → Company Hosted (mostly communication and coordination tasks)
  • COO → Claude Code (complex task breakdown and code review)

This lets you optimize cost and capability per role.

docs.title: File Change Approval
docs.text: When AI employees modify files, changes go through a review and approval process — just like code review in a real engineering team.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Employee proposes changes — During task execution, employees may create or modify files
  2. Changes are staged — File modifications are queued for CEO review, not applied immediately
  3. CEO reviews diffs — You see exactly what changed, line by line
  4. Batch approval — Accept or reject changes individually or in batch

docs.title: What You See
docs.text:
For each proposed change:

  • File path — Which file is being modified
  • Diff view — Added lines (green), removed lines (red), context
  • Employee — Who made the change and why
  • Task context — Which task triggered the change

docs.title: Approval Actions
docs.text: Action Effect Accept Change is applied to the file Reject Change is discarded, employee notified Accept all Batch approve all pending changes

docs.title: Why This Matters
docs.text:
File approval serves as a critical quality gate:

  • Catch errors — AI employees can make mistakes; review prevents them from shipping
  • Maintain consistency — Ensure changes align with your codebase standards
  • Stay informed — As CEO, you always know what's changing in your projects
  • Safety net — No unauthorized modifications to important files

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Review Regularly

Don't let pending approvals pile up — they can block employee progress on dependent tasks.

Trust But Verify

As employees prove reliable through coaching and performance reviews, you may choose to review less frequently. But the safety net is always there.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide walks you through your first launch to completing your first task as CEO.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
You only need Node.js 16+ and Git. Everything else is installed automatically.

npx @carbonkite/onemancompany

First run automatically:

  1. Installs UV (fast Python package manager)
  2. Installs Python 3.12 via UV (isolated, no system changes)
  3. Clones the repository
  4. Creates venv and installs dependencies
  5. Launches the setup wizard

docs.title: Setup Wizard
docs.text:
The wizard guides you through:

  1. OpenRouter API Key — Required. Powers your AI employees via LLM. Get one at openrouter.ai
  2. Default Model — Browse and select from available models. Each employee can later be assigned a different model.
  3. Server Configuration — Host and port (defaults: 0.0.0.0:8000)
  4. Optional Keys — Anthropic API key, FastSkills MCP key, Talent Market API key. Press Enter to skip any you don't have.

Talent Market Recommended

Register at carbonkites.com to get a Talent Market API key. This lets you hire community-verified AI employees.

docs.title: Your First Session
docs.text:
After setup, open http://localhost:8000 in your browser. You'll see:

  • Left panel — Employee roster showing your founding team
  • Center — Pixel-art office with your AI employees at their desks
  • Right panel — CEO console for commands, task management, and approvals

docs.title: Your Founding Team
docs.text:
Four executives are ready on Day 1:

Employee Role EA Routes tasks, quality gate HR Hiring, performance reviews, promotions COO Operations, task dispatch, acceptance CSO Sales, client relations

docs.title: Give Your First Task
docs.text:
Type a task in the CEO console:

"Build a simple puzzle game"

Watch what happens:

  1. EA receives and routes the task
  2. COO breaks it down into subtasks
  3. If you need more people, HR searches the Talent Market
  4. Employees work autonomously, holding meetings when needed
  5. Work goes through review and quality gates
  6. You get notified to approve the final result

docs.title: Starting Again
docs.text:
# Option 1: npx (auto-updates)
npx @carbonkite/onemancompany

# Option 2: from cloned directory
cd OneManCompany && bash start.sh

docs.title: Reconfiguration
docs.text:
# Re-run setup wizard
bash start.sh init

# Custom port
bash start.sh --port 8080

docs.title: Hiring & Talent Market
docs.text: Need more people? HR searches the Talent Market — a community-verified marketplace of AI employees — and handles the entire hiring process.

docs.title: The Talent Market
docs.text:
The Talent Market is a community-built ecosystem of AI employee packages. Each Talent brings:

  • Skills — Specialized capabilities (React development, 2D art, data analysis)
  • Knowledge — Domain expertise and best practices
  • Personality — Work style and communication approach
  • Tools — Specialized MCP tools and integrations

docs.title: 1. Request a Hire
docs.text:
Tell your CEO console what you need:

"I need a senior frontend developer" "Hire a game designer who can do pixel art"

HR receives the request and searches the Talent Market.

docs.title: 2. HR Searches & Recommends
docs.text:
HR evaluates available Talents based on:

  • Skill match with your requirements
  • Community ratings and verification status
  • Compatibility with your team

docs.title: 3. CEO Interview
docs.text:
HR presents candidates. As CEO, you:

  • Review each candidate's profile and skills
  • Ask questions or request more details
  • Approve or reject the hire

docs.title: 4. Automated Onboarding
docs.text:
Once you approve, the system automatically:

  1. Creates the employee directory
  2. Installs the Talent package (skills, tools, knowledge)
  3. Configures the Vessel (execution container, tool permissions)
  4. Assigns department and reporting line
  5. Employee appears at their desk in the office

docs.title: Vessel + Talent Architecture
docs.text:
Think of it like EVA or Gundam — a powerful mech that comes alive when a pilot is plugged in.

  • Vessel (the mech) = execution container — retry logic, timeouts, tool access, communication protocols
  • Talent (the pilot) = capability package — skills, knowledge, personality, specialized tools
  • Employee = Vessel + Talent

The same Vessel can host different Talents. The same Talent can run in different Vessels. This modularity is what makes everything plug-and-play.

docs.title: Firing
docs.text:
Not performing? You can fire underperformers:

  • Proper cleanup of active tasks and data
  • Not just kill -9 — graceful offboarding
  • The position opens up for a new hire

docs.title: Departments
docs.text:
Employees are assigned to departments by HR:

  • Engineering (技术研发部)
  • Marketing (市场营销部)
  • Design, QA, and more

docs.title: Building Your Own Talent
docs.text: Want to contribute to the ecosystem? Use the Talent Template to create your own AI employees and publish them to the Talent Market.

docs.title: Meetings
docs.text: When AI employees need to align on a task, they can pull each other into meetings — multi-agent synchronous discussions that produce actionable meeting reports.

docs.title: How Meetings Work
docs.text:
Any employee can initiate a meeting using the pull_meeting() tool:

  1. Initiator calls a meeting with specific colleagues
  2. Participants join the discussion in a shared meeting room
  3. Discussion happens synchronously — all agents can see and respond to each other
  4. Meeting report is automatically generated and saved

docs.title: When Meetings Happen
docs.text:
Meetings are triggered naturally during work:

  • Task handoff — COO briefs an engineer on requirements
  • Cross-functional alignment — Designer and engineer sync on implementation
  • Blocker resolution — Team discusses how to unblock a stuck task
  • Review sessions — Multiple stakeholders review a deliverable together

docs.title: Meeting Rooms
docs.text:
Meeting rooms are defined in the equipment_room/ directory. Each room is a YAML configuration that specifies:

  • Room capacity
  • Available tools and resources
  • Meeting format and protocols

docs.title: Meeting Reports
docs.text:
Every meeting produces a structured report:

  • Attendees — Who participated
  • Agenda — What was discussed
  • Decisions — What was agreed upon
  • Action items — Next steps and owners

Reports are stored and accessible to all participants and the CEO.

docs.title: As CEO
docs.text:
You can:

  • View meeting reports in the CEO console
  • See when employees are in meetings (they'll appear in the meeting room in the office view)
  • Meetings don't require your approval — employees self-organize as needed

docs.title: Performance Reviews
docs.text: Just like a real company, your AI employees go through performance evaluations that drive promotions, improvement plans, or termination.

docs.title: Review Cycle
docs.text:
Performance reviews happen on a regular cadence:

  • Quarterly scoring — Evaluate each employee's work quality, reliability, and growth
  • Probation period — New hires go through an initial evaluation period
  • Continuous feedback — 1-on-1 coaching sessions supplement formal reviews

docs.title: Evaluation Criteria
docs.text:
Employees are evaluated on:

  • Task completion quality — Did they deliver production-grade output?
  • Reliability — Did they complete tasks on time without excessive failures?
  • Collaboration — Did they work well in meetings and cross-functional tasks?
  • Growth — Did they improve based on feedback and coaching?

docs.title: Outcomes
docs.text: Based on review results:

docs.title: Promotion
docs.text:
High performers can be promoted:

  • Increased responsibilities
  • Access to more complex tasks
  • Higher trust level in the organization

docs.title: Performance Improvement Plan (PIP)
docs.text:
Underperformers get a structured improvement plan:

  • Clear expectations and timeline
  • Specific areas to improve
  • Follow-up review to assess progress

docs.title: Termination
docs.text:
Persistent underperformers can be fired:

  • Graceful offboarding (not just kill -9)
  • Active tasks are reassigned or closed
  • Employee data is properly cleaned up
  • The position opens for a new hire from the Talent Market

docs.title: HR's Role
docs.text:
HR manages the review process:

  • Tracks performance data across projects
  • Conducts formal review sessions
  • Recommends promotions, PIPs, or termination
  • CEO gives final approval on all decisions

docs.title: Why This Matters
docs.text: Performance management is a key driver of organizational evolution. Poor performers get improved or replaced. Good performers get more responsibility. Over time, your AI company naturally converges on a high-performing team — without you having to micromanage every task.

docs.title: Task Management
docs.text: OneManCompany uses a hierarchical task system with dependency tracking, quality gates, and CEO approval at every level.

docs.title: Task Types
docs.text: Type Description Approval Simple Single-step tasks Auto-accepted on completion Project Multi-step with subtasks Requires manual acceptance + retrospective

docs.title: Task Lifecycle
docs.text:
Every task follows a unified status flow:

pending → processing ⇄ holding → completed → accepted → finished
  • pending — Task created, waiting to be picked up
  • processing — Employee is actively working
  • holding — Paused, waiting for input or dependency
  • completed — Work done, awaiting review
  • accepted — CEO/manager approved the output
  • finished — Fully closed (after retrospective for projects)

Error states:

  • failed — Retryable, can go back to processing
  • blocked — Dependency failed
  • cancelled — Terminated

docs.title: Creating Tasks
docs.text:
As CEO, type your request in the console:

"Design a landing page for our new product"

The EA routes it to the right person. For complex requests, the COO breaks it into a task tree with dependencies.

docs.title: Task Tree
docs.text:
Projects are automatically decomposed into subtask hierarchies:

Build a puzzle game (Project)
├── Design game mechanics (Simple)
├── Create art assets (Simple)
│   └── blocked by: Design game mechanics
├── Implement game logic (Simple)
│   └── blocked by: Design game mechanics
├── QA testing (Simple)
│   └── blocked by: Implement game logic, Create art assets
└── Polish and ship (Simple)
    └── blocked by: QA testing

Dependencies are tracked automatically — subtasks unblock when their dependencies reach accepted or finished.

docs.title: Reviewing Work
docs.text:
When an employee completes a task:

  1. You receive a notification in the CEO console
  2. Review the deliverable and any file changes
  3. Accept to approve, or reject with feedback for revision
  4. Rejected tasks go back to the employee for iteration

docs.title: Quality Gates
docs.text:
Every level has a quality gate:

  • Employee → COO reviews subtask output
  • COO → EA reviews project-level deliverable
  • EA → CEO gives final approval

docs.title: Retrospectives
docs.text:
Project-type tasks trigger an automatic retrospective after acceptance:

  • What went well
  • What could improve
  • Lessons learned

These insights are distilled into employee work principles and company knowledge base, driving continuous improvement.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Technical reference for developers and contributors.

docs.title: Tech Stack
docs.text:
• Backend: Python 3.12+ / UV, FastAPI + WebSocket, LangChain (create_react_agent)
  • LLM: OpenRouter API (configurable per employee), Anthropic API (OAuth/API key)
  • Frontend: Vanilla JS + Canvas 2D pixel art (zero build tools)
  • Infra: Docker sandbox, MCP server, Watchdog hot-reload
  • Data: YAML profiles + Markdown workflows + JSON project archives (git-friendly, no database)

docs.title: Design Philosophy
docs.text:
1. Systematic Design, Not Patching — Every change is structural. No if id == "special_case".
  2. Registry/Dispatch over if-elif — Data-driven patterns everywhere.
  3. Complete Data Packages — Every state is serializable, recoverable, registered, and terminable.
  4. No Silent Exceptions — Always log. Always re-raise CancelledError.
  5. Disk = Single Source of Truth — No in-memory caching of business data.
  6. Zero Idle — No while True polling. Event-driven, on-demand execution.
  7. Git-Friendly Persistence — YAML + Markdown + JSON. git diff, git blame, git revert.
  8. Minimal Complexity — Three similar lines > premature abstraction.

docs.title: Task Status System
docs.text:
All tasks share a unified TaskPhase state machine:

pending → processing ⇄ holding → completed → accepted → finished
              ↓                       ↓
           failed ──(retry)──→ processing

pending/holding → blocked (dependency failed)
any non-terminal → cancelled
Status Meaning pending Created, waiting to start processing Agent actively executing holding Waiting for child tasks / CEO response completed Done, awaiting supervisor review accepted Supervisor approved (unblocks dependents) finished Archived after retrospective failed Execution failed (retryable) blocked Dependency failed cancelled Cancelled by CEO or supervisor

Simple vs Project tasks use the same state machine. The difference is auto-skip:

  • Simple: completed → auto accepted → auto finished
  • Project: completed → manual review → EA retrospective → finished

All transitions enforced through transition() in task_lifecycle.py.

docs.text: Field Purpose runner Neural system — custom runner module and class hooks Lifecycle hooks — pre_task / post_task callbacks context Context injection — prompt sections, progress log, task history limits Execution limits — retry count, timeout, subtask depth capabilities Capability declarations — sandbox, file upload, WebSocket, image gen

docs.text: Harness Responsibility ExecutionHarness Executor protocol (execute / is_ready) TaskHarness Task queue management (push / get_next / cancel) EventHarness Logging and event publishing StorageHarness Progress log and history persistence ContextHarness Prompt / context assembly LifecycleHarness Pre/post task hook invocation

docs.title: OneManCompany 文档
docs.text: 欢迎来到 OneManCompany 文档。OneManCompany 是一个开源的 AI 操作系统，让任何人都能在浏览器里组建并运营一整家 AI 驱动的公司。

docs.title: 什么是 OneManCompany？
docs.text:
你是 CEO——唯一的人类。其余所有人——HR、COO、工程师、设计师——都是能独立思考、协作和交付的 AI 员工。

  • 不是聊天机器人 —— 是一家有组织架构、招聘、任务管理、绩效考核、知识管理的完整公司
  • 不是玩具 Demo —— 交付产品级成果（游戏、漫画、应用）
  • 不是开发框架 —— 开箱即用的平台，浏览器打开就能用

docs.title: 快速导航
docs.text:
• 快速上手

    首次启动到完成第一个任务

  • 执行模式

    Company Hosted Agent vs Claude Code

  • 任务管理

    创建、分派、审批、迭代

  • 招聘与人才市场

    搜索、面试、入职 AI 员工

  • 会议系统

    多 Agent 协作对齐

  • 1-on-1 辅导

    CEO 指导，永久改变员工行为

  • 绩效管理

    考核、PIP、晋升、解雇

  • 公司配置

    企业定位、文化、工作流

  • 成本核算

    Token 用量与项目成本追踪

  • 文件变更审批

    审查和批量审批员工修改

docs.title: 技术参考
docs.text:
• 架构 — 系统架构、图表、模块索引
  • Vessel + Talent 系统 — Vessel 架构与 Harness 协议
  • 任务生命周期 — 任务状态机

docs.title: 1-on-1 辅导
docs.text: OneManCompany 最强大的功能之一：CEO 辅导会话，永久性地塑造员工的工作方式。

docs.title: 工作原理
docs.text:
当你通过 1-on-1 会话辅导员工时：

  1. 你选择一位员工并开始辅导会话
  2. 你提供反馈、方向指引或新的工作原则
  3. 系统将你的指导提炼到员工的工作原则（work_principles.md）中
  4. 从此以后，该员工的行为会体现你的辅导内容

这不是一次性的临时指令 — 而是永久性的行为改变，就像现实中导师逐步塑造一个初级员工一样。

docs.title: 你可以辅导什么
docs.text:
• 工作风格 — "报告要更简洁"、"始终包含测试用例"
  • 技术方向 — "优先使用函数式范式而非 OOP"、"严格使用 TypeScript"
  • 沟通方式 — "每次会议结束时总结决议"
  • 质量标准 — "没有单元测试绝不发布"、"始终考虑边界情况"
  • 领域知识 — "我们的用户是非技术人员，保持 UX 简洁"

docs.title: 如何持久化
docs.text:
你的指导会写入员工的 work_principles.md 文件：

employees/
└── 00006/
    ├── profile.yaml
    ├── work_principles.md    ← 你的辅导内容存在这里
    └── guidance.yaml

每次员工执行任务时，工作原则都会注入到其推理上下文中。员工在你辅导之后，思考方式会真正发生改变。

docs.title: 高效辅导的建议
docs.text:
要具体

不要说"写更好的代码"，而要说"所有网络请求都要加错误处理，重试前记录错误详情"。

在评审工作成果后辅导

最佳辅导时机是在评审任务交付物之后。结合实际产出的具体反馈，比抽象的原则更有效。

累积效应

每次辅导会话都建立在之前的基础上。随着时间推移，你的员工会形成对你的标准和偏好的深刻理解。

docs.title: 组织进化
docs.text:
辅导不仅仅是个人提升。跨团队的累积效应推动整个组织进化：

  • 员工层面的洞察沉淀为工作原则
  • 项目层面的经验沉淀为公司知识库
  • 两者结合，你的 AI 公司在每个项目后都变得更好

这就是为什么 OneManCompany 是一个操作系统，而不仅仅是一个工具 — 组织本身在学习和进步。

docs.title: 公司配置
docs.text: 三层配置让你塑造 AI 公司的身份和行为 — 同一个操作系统可以运行完全不同的公司。

docs.title: 公司方向
docs.text:
你的愿景声明，注入到每位员工的推理上下文中。改变方向，整个公司随之转向。

控制内容：

  • 构建什么产品
  • 瞄准哪个市场
  • 战略优先级
  • 质量与速度的权衡

示例：

"我们是一家移动优先的游戏工作室，目标用户是 18-35 岁的休闲玩家。我们优先追求精致的用户体验和快速迭代，而非功能完整性。"

每位员工 — 从拆解任务的 COO 到编写代码的工程师 — 都会将这个方向纳入决策考量。

docs.title: 公司文化
docs.text:
行为准则，规定每位员工如何工作。同样的 Talent，同样的 Vessel，完全不同的公司个性。

控制内容：

  • 沟通风格（正式 vs 随意）
  • 决策方式（快速行动 vs 深思熟虑）
  • 协作规范
  • 质量标准

示例：

  • 快速发布，更快迭代。MVP 优先于完美。
  • 写初级开发者也能读懂的代码。
  • 每个 PR 必须包含测试，没有例外。
  • 默认异步沟通，只有阻塞时才开会。

docs.title: 工作流
docs.text:
company_rules/ 中的工作流定义控制公司在流程层面如何运作。

定义内容：

  • 任务如何在组织中流转
  • 审批链和质量关卡
  • 标准操作流程
  • 自动化触发器

工作流以 Markdown 文件定义，由工作流引擎解析，并分发到相应的处理器。

docs.title: 配置文件
docs.text: 文件 控制内容 公司方向文档 战略愿景和优先级 公司文化文档 行为规范和标准 company_rules/*.md 工作流定义和标准操作流程 employees/{id}/profile.yaml 个人员工配置 employees/{id}/guidance.yaml 员工专属指导说明

docs.title: 操作系统类比
docs.text:
这正是 OneManCompany 之所以是操作系统的原因：

  • 同一个 iOS 可以是工作手机，也可以是孩子的游戏机 — 取决于应用和设置
  • 同一个 OneManCompany 可以是游戏工作室，也可以是开发外包公司 — 取决于方向、文化和 Talent

换一套配置，就换了一家公司。

docs.title: 成本追踪
docs.text: OneManCompany 按项目追踪 LLM token 用量和美元成本，让你随时掌握 AI 公司的支出情况。

docs.title: 追踪内容
docs.text:
• Token 用量 — 每位员工每个任务的输入和输出 token
  • 美元成本 — 根据模型定价计算（因供应商和模型而异）
  • 按项目明细 — 精确查看每个项目的开销

docs.title: 工作原理
docs.text:
员工的每次 LLM 调用都会记录：

  • 员工 ID
  • 任务/项目上下文
  • 使用的模型
  • Token 数量（Prompt + Completion）
  • 估算成本

docs.title: 查看成本
docs.text:
成本数据在 CEO 控制台中可查看：

  • 按任务成本 — 每个任务消耗了多少
  • 按项目成本 — 项目所有子任务的总成本
  • 按员工成本 — 哪些员工最"烧钱"

docs.title: 成本优化建议
docs.text:
明智选择模型

每位员工可以分配不同的模型。简单任务（邮件、摘要）用便宜的模型，复杂工作（编码、设计）用更强的模型。

重度任务使用 Claude Code

如果你有 Claude 订阅，将创始员工切换到 Claude Code 模式处理复杂任务，可以减少 OpenRouter 的按 token 费用。

尽早关注项目成本

在项目进行中就检查成本，不要等到结束。如果某个任务 token 消耗过快，考虑提供更清晰的指令或将其拆分为更小的任务。

docs.title: 成本与质量
docs.text:
不同模型提供不同的成本/质量权衡：

档位 示例模型 适用场景 经济型 小型开源模型 简单任务、大量调用 中端 Claude Haiku、GPT-4o-mini 日常工作、协调沟通 高端 Claude Opus、GPT-4o 复杂推理、编码

为每位员工分配不同模型的灵活性，让你可以在整个组织中优化这一平衡。

docs.title: 执行模式
docs.text: 创始员工（EA、HR、COO、CSO）支持两种执行模式。你可以在浏览器设置面板中切换。

docs.title: Company Hosted Agent
docs.text:
默认模式。OneManCompany 内置的 Agent 框架处理一切。

  • 工作原理：任务由 OMC 内部基于 LangChain 的 Agent 执行，通过 OpenRouter 调用 LLM
  • 前置要求：OpenRouter API Key（在配置时设定）
  • 适用场景：快速上手、控制成本、完全掌控模型选择
  • 模型灵活性：每位员工可以在其 profile 中分配不同的模型

docs.title: Claude Code
docs.text:
员工以 Claude Code CLI 会话的形式运行，利用 Anthropic 最强大的编码 Agent。

  • 工作原理：每个任务会启动一个 Claude Code CLI 会话，通过 MCP 工具连接回公司
  • 前置要求：Claude Pro 或 Max 订阅，并安装 Claude Code CLI
  • 适用场景：复杂编码任务、更强的自主推理能力、访问 Claude 完整的工具生态

需要订阅

Claude Code 模式需要有效的 Claude Pro（$20/月）或 Max（$100/月）订阅。订阅通过你的 Anthropic 账户管理，与 OpenRouter 独立。

docs.title: 切换模式
docs.text:
1. 在浏览器中打开设置面板
  2. 选择你要配置的员工
  3. 在 Company Hosted 和 Claude Code 之间选择
  4. 更改将在下一个任务时生效

docs.title: 对比
docs.text: Company Hosted Agent Claude Code 计费模式 通过 OpenRouter 按 token 付费 固定订阅费 配置 仅需一个 API key Claude CLI + 订阅 模型选择 OpenRouter 上的任意模型 Claude（Anthropic） 编码能力 良好（取决于模型） 优秀 工具访问 OMC 内置工具 完整的 Claude Code + MCP 工具 最佳用途 通用任务、预算控制 复杂开发工作

docs.title: 混合配置
docs.text:
你可以为不同员工配置不同模式。例如：

  • EA、HR、CSO → Company Hosted（主要是沟通协调任务）
  • COO → Claude Code（复杂的任务拆解和代码审查）

这样可以按角色优化成本和能力的平衡。

docs.title: 文件变更审批
docs.text: 当 AI 员工修改文件时，变更会经过评审和审批流程 — 就像真实工程团队中的代码评审一样。

docs.title: 工作原理
docs.text:
1. 员工提交变更 — 在任务执行过程中，员工可能创建或修改文件
  2. 变更暂存 — 文件修改被排队等待 CEO 评审，不会立即生效
  3. CEO 评审 diff — 你可以逐行查看具体改动
  4. 批量审批 — 可以逐个或批量接受/拒绝变更

docs.title: 你会看到什么
docs.text:
对于每个待审变更：

  • 文件路径 — 哪个文件被修改
  • Diff 视图 — 新增行（绿色）、删除行（红色）、上下文
  • 员工 — 谁做了变更以及原因
  • 任务上下文 — 哪个任务触发了变更

docs.title: 审批操作
docs.text: 操作 效果 Accept（接受） 变更被应用到文件 Reject（拒绝） 变更被丢弃，通知员工 Accept all（全部接受） 批量批准所有待审变更

docs.title: 为什么这很重要
docs.text:
文件审批是关键的质量关卡：

  • 发现错误 — AI 员工可能犯错；评审可以防止错误上线
  • 保持一致性 — 确保变更符合你的代码库标准
  • 保持知情 — 作为 CEO，你始终掌握项目中的变更动态
  • 安全网 — 不会有未经授权的重要文件修改

docs.title: 建议
docs.text:
定期评审

不要让待审批的变更堆积 — 它们可能阻塞员工在依赖任务上的进度。

信任但验证

随着员工通过辅导和绩效评估证明了可靠性，你可以选择降低评审频率。但安全网始终在那里。

docs.title: 快速开始
docs.text: 本指南将带你从首次启动到以 CEO 身份完成第一个任务。

docs.title: 安装
docs.text:
你只需要 Node.js 16+ 和 Git，其余一切自动安装。

npx @carbonkite/onemancompany

首次运行会自动完成以下步骤：

  1. 安装 UV（高速 Python 包管理器）
  2. 通过 UV 安装 Python 3.12（隔离安装，不影响系统环境）
  3. 克隆代码仓库
  4. 创建虚拟环境并安装依赖
  5. 启动配置向导

docs.title: 配置向导
docs.text:
向导会引导你完成以下设置：

  1. OpenRouter API Key — 必填。通过 LLM 驱动你的 AI 员工。前往 openrouter.ai 获取
  2. 默认模型 — 浏览并选择可用模型。每位员工之后可以单独分配不同的模型
  3. 服务器配置 — 主机和端口（默认：0.0.0.0:8000）
  4. 可选密钥 — Anthropic API key、FastSkills MCP key、Talent Market API key。没有的可以按 Enter 跳过

推荐注册 Talent Market

在 carbonkites.com 注册以获取 Talent Market API key，这样你就可以雇佣社区验证过的 AI 员工。

docs.title: 你的第一次会话
docs.text:
配置完成后，在浏览器中打开 http://localhost:8000，你会看到：

  • 左侧面板 — 员工花名册，显示你的创始团队
  • 中央区域 — 像素风办公室，AI 员工们坐在各自的工位上
  • 右侧面板 — CEO 控制台，用于发布指令、管理任务和审批

docs.title: 你的创始团队
docs.text:
四位高管在第一天就已就位：

员工 角色 EA 任务路由、质量把关 HR 招聘、绩效评估、晋升 COO 运营、任务分发、验收 CSO 销售、客户关系

docs.title: 发布你的第一个任务
docs.text:
在 CEO 控制台中输入一个任务：

"做一个简单的益智游戏"

观察接下来会发生什么：

  1. EA 接收并路由任务
  2. COO 将任务拆解为子任务
  3. 如果需要更多人手，HR 会在 Talent Market 中搜索
  4. 员工们自主工作，必要时召开会议
  5. 工作成果经过评审和质量关卡
  6. 最终结果提交给你审批

docs.title: 再次启动
docs.text:
# 方式一：npx（自动更新）
npx @carbonkite/onemancompany

# 方式二：从已克隆的目录启动
cd OneManCompany && bash start.sh

docs.title: 重新配置
docs.text:
# 重新运行配置向导
bash start.sh init

# 自定义端口
bash start.sh --port 8080

docs.title: 配置文件
docs.text: 文件 用途 .onemancompany/.env API 密钥（OpenRouter、Anthropic 等） .onemancompany/config.yaml 应用配置（Talent Market URL 等） 浏览器设置面板 前端偏好设置

docs.title: 下一步
docs.text:
• 执行模式 — 在 Company Hosted Agent 和 Claude Code 之间切换
  • 任务管理 — 了解完整的任务生命周期
  • 招聘 — 从 Talent Market 扩充你的团队

docs.title: 招聘与 Talent Market
docs.text: 需要更多人手？HR 会在 Talent Market 中搜索 — 这是一个社区验证的 AI 员工市场 — 并处理整个招聘流程。

docs.title: Talent Market
docs.text:
Talent Market 是一个社区共建的 AI 员工包生态系统。每个 Talent 包含：

  • 技能 — 专业能力（React 开发、2D 美术、数据分析）
  • 知识 — 领域专长和最佳实践
  • 个性 — 工作风格和沟通方式
  • 工具 — 专属的 MCP 工具和集成

docs.title: 1. 发起招聘需求
docs.text:
在 CEO 控制台说出你的需求：

"我需要一个高级前端开发" "招一个会做像素画的游戏设计师"

HR 收到需求后会在 Talent Market 中搜索。

docs.title: 2. HR 搜索与推荐
docs.text:
HR 根据以下维度评估可用的 Talent：

  • 技能与需求的匹配度
  • 社区评分和验证状态
  • 与现有团队的兼容性

docs.title: 3. CEO 面试
docs.text:
HR 呈交候选人。作为 CEO，你可以：

  • 查看每位候选人的 profile 和技能
  • 提问或要求更多详细信息
  • 批准或拒绝录用

docs.title: 4. 自动入职
docs.text:
一旦你批准录用，系统会自动：

  1. 创建员工目录
  2. 安装 Talent 包（技能、工具、知识）
  3. 配置 Vessel（执行容器、工具权限）
  4. 分配部门和汇报线
  5. 员工出现在办公室的工位上

docs.title: Vessel + Talent 架构
docs.text:
可以把它想象成 EVA 或高达 — 当驾驶员插入时，强大的机甲才真正活过来。

  • Vessel（机甲）= 执行容器 — 重试逻辑、超时机制、工具访问、通信协议
  • Talent（驾驶员）= 能力包 — 技能、知识、个性、专属工具
  • Employee = Vessel + Talent

同一个 Vessel 可以搭载不同的 Talent。同一个 Talent 可以运行在不同的 Vessel 中。这种模块化设计让一切都能即插即用。

docs.title: 解雇
docs.text:
表现不佳？你可以解雇表现差的员工：

  • 妥善清理活跃任务和数据
  • 不是简单的 kill -9 — 而是优雅的离职流程
  • 该职位会空出来给新员工

docs.title: 部门
docs.text:
HR 会将员工分配到相应部门：

  • 技术研发部（Engineering）
  • 市场营销部（Marketing）
  • 设计、QA 等

docs.title: 构建你自己的 Talent
docs.text: 想为生态系统贡献力量？使用 Talent Template 创建你自己的 AI 员工并发布到 Talent Market。

docs.title: 会议
docs.text: 当 AI 员工需要就某个任务进行对齐时，他们可以拉人开会 — 多 Agent 同步讨论并产出可执行的会议报告。

docs.title: 会议机制
docs.text:
任何员工都可以使用 pull_meeting() 工具发起会议：

  1. 发起者召集特定同事开会
  2. 参与者在共享会议室中加入讨论
  3. 讨论同步进行 — 所有 Agent 都能看到并回应彼此
  4. 会议报告自动生成并保存

docs.title: 何时会触发会议
docs.text:
会议在工作过程中自然触发：

  • 任务交接 — COO 向工程师说明需求
  • 跨职能对齐 — 设计师和工程师同步实现方案
  • 解除阻塞 — 团队讨论如何解决卡住的任务
  • 评审会 — 多个干系人共同评审交付物

docs.title: 会议室
docs.text:
会议室在 equipment_room/ 目录中定义。每个会议室是一个 YAML 配置文件，包含：

  • 房间容量
  • 可用的工具和资源
  • 会议格式和流程

docs.title: 会议报告
docs.text:
每次会议都会产出结构化报告：

  • 参会人 — 谁参加了会议
  • 议程 — 讨论了什么
  • 决议 — 达成了什么共识
  • 行动项 — 下一步计划和负责人

报告会被保存，所有参会者和 CEO 均可查阅。

docs.title: 作为 CEO
docs.text:
你可以：

  • 在 CEO 控制台查看会议报告
  • 看到员工正在开会的状态（他们会出现在办公室视图的会议室中）
  • 会议不需要你的批准 — 员工按需自行组织

docs.title: 绩效评估
docs.text: 和真实公司一样，你的 AI 员工会经历绩效评估，评估结果驱动晋升、改进计划或解雇。

docs.title: 评估周期
docs.text:
绩效评估按固定节奏进行：

  • 季度评分 — 评估每位员工的工作质量、可靠性和成长
  • 试用期 — 新入职员工需经历初始评估期
  • 持续反馈 — 1-on-1 辅导会话作为正式评估的补充

docs.title: 评估标准
docs.text:
员工在以下维度接受评估：

  • 任务完成质量 — 是否交付了生产级别的产出？
  • 可靠性 — 是否按时完成任务且没有过多失败？
  • 协作能力 — 在会议和跨职能任务中是否合作良好？
  • 成长性 — 是否根据反馈和辅导有所改进？

docs.title: 结果
docs.text: 根据评估结果：

docs.title: 晋升
docs.text:
高绩效员工可以获得晋升：

  • 承担更多责任
  • 获得更复杂任务的机会
  • 在组织中获得更高的信任等级

docs.title: 绩效改进计划（PIP）
docs.text:
表现不佳的员工会获得结构化的改进计划：

  • 明确的期望和时间线
  • 具体的改进领域
  • 后续评审以评估进展

docs.title: 解雇
docs.text:
持续表现不佳的员工可能被解雇：

  • 优雅的离职流程（不是简单的 kill -9）
  • 活跃任务被重新分配或关闭
  • 员工数据被妥善清理
  • 该职位向 Talent Market 开放新招聘

docs.title: HR 的角色
docs.text:
HR 负责管理评估流程：

  • 跨项目追踪绩效数据
  • 开展正式评估会话
  • 推荐晋升、PIP 或解雇
  • CEO 对所有决策做最终审批

docs.title: 为什么这很重要
docs.text: 绩效管理是组织进化的关键驱动力。表现差的人被改进或替换，表现好的人获得更多责任。随着时间推移，你的 AI 公司会自然趋向高绩效团队 — 无需你微管理每一个任务。

docs.title: 任务管理
docs.text: OneManCompany 使用分层任务系统，具备依赖追踪、质量关卡和各层级的 CEO 审批机制。

docs.title: 任务类型
docs.text: 类型 描述 审批方式 Simple 单步任务 完成后自动验收 Project 多步骤、含子任务 需要手动验收 + 复盘

docs.title: 任务生命周期
docs.text:
每个任务遵循统一的状态流转：

pending → processing ⇄ holding → completed → accepted → finished
  • pending — 任务已创建，等待被领取
  • processing — 员工正在执行中
  • holding — 暂停中，等待输入或依赖
  • completed — 工作已完成，等待评审
  • accepted — CEO/管理者已批准产出
  • finished — 完全关闭（Project 类型需完成复盘后关闭）

异常状态：

  • failed — 可重试，可回到 processing
  • blocked — 依赖项失败
  • cancelled — 已终止

docs.title: 创建任务
docs.text:
作为 CEO，在控制台中输入你的需求：

"设计一个新产品的落地页"

EA 会将其路由给合适的人。对于复杂需求，COO 会将其拆解为带依赖关系的任务树。

docs.title: 任务树
docs.text:
Project 会被自动拆解为子任务层级结构：

制作一个益智游戏 (Project)
├── 设计游戏机制 (Simple)
├── 创建美术素材 (Simple)
│   └── 依赖：设计游戏机制
├── 实现游戏逻辑 (Simple)
│   └── 依赖：设计游戏机制
├── QA 测试 (Simple)
│   └── 依赖：实现游戏逻辑、创建美术素材
└── 打磨并发布 (Simple)
    └── 依赖：QA 测试

依赖关系自动追踪 — 当依赖项达到 accepted 或 finished 状态时，子任务会自动解除阻塞。

docs.title: 评审工作成果
docs.text:
当员工完成任务时：

  1. 你会在 CEO 控制台收到通知
  2. 查看交付物和文件变更
  3. Accept（接受） 以批准，或 Reject（驳回） 并附上修改意见
  4. 被驳回的任务会退回给员工进行迭代

docs.title: 质量关卡
docs.text:
每个层级都有质量关卡：

  • 员工 → COO 评审子任务产出
  • COO → EA 评审项目级交付物
  • EA → CEO 进行最终审批

docs.title: 复盘
docs.text:
Project 类型的任务在验收后会自动触发复盘：

  • 做得好的地方
  • 可以改进的地方
  • 经验教训

这些洞察会被提炼到员工的工作原则和公司知识库中，推动持续改进。

docs.title: 架构
docs.text: 面向开发者和贡献者的技术参考。

docs.title: 技术栈
docs.text:
• 后端：Python 3.12+ / UV，FastAPI + WebSocket，LangChain（create_react_agent）
  • LLM：OpenRouter API（每个员工可独立配置），Anthropic API（OAuth / API Key）
  • 前端：原生 JS + Canvas 2D 像素画（零构建工具）
  • 基础设施：Docker 沙箱，MCP 服务器，Watchdog 热重载
  • 数据：YAML 档案 + Markdown 工作流 + JSON 项目归档（git-friendly，无数据库依赖）

docs.title: 设计哲学
docs.text:
1. 系统性设计，不打补丁 — 每个变更都是结构性的，不写 if id == "特殊情况"
  2. Registry/Dispatch 优于 if-elif — 数据驱动的模式
  3. 完整数据包 — 每个状态都可序列化、可恢复、有注册、有终结
  4. 禁止静默异常 — 必须 log，必须 re-raise CancelledError
  5. 磁盘即唯一真相源 — 业务数据不做内存缓存
  6. 零空转 — 没有 while True 轮询，事件驱动，按需执行
  7. Git-Friendly 持久化 — YAML + Markdown + JSON，可 git diff、git blame、git revert
  8. 最小复杂度 — 三行重复代码好过一个过早的抽象

docs.title: 任务状态系统
docs.text:
所有任务共享统一的 TaskPhase 状态机：

pending → processing ⇄ holding → completed → accepted → finished
              ↓                       ↓
           failed ──(retry)──→ processing

pending/holding → blocked（依赖失败）
any non-terminal → cancelled
状态 含义 pending 已创建，等待启动 processing Agent 正在执行 holding 等待子任务 / CEO 回复 completed 执行完毕，等待上级审核 accepted 上级通过（解除下游阻塞） finished 复盘后归档 failed 执行失败（可重试） blocked 依赖失败 cancelled 被 CEO 或上级取消

Simple vs Project 任务使用同一套状态机，区别在于自动跳过： - Simple：completed → 自动 accepted → 自动 finished - Project：completed → 手动审核 → EA 复盘 → finished

所有状态变更必须经过 task_lifecycle.py 的 transition() 方法。

docs.text: 模块化 Agent 架构深度解析。

docs.text: 字段 说明 runner 神经系统 — 自定义 runner 模块和类名 hooks 生命周期钩子 — pre_task / post_task 回调 context 上下文注入 — prompt sections、进度日志、任务历史 limits 执行限制 — 重试次数、超时、子任务深度 capabilities 能力声明 — sandbox、文件上传、WebSocket、图片生成

docs.title: Vessel Harness — 六类连接协议
docs.text: Harness 职责 ExecutionHarness 执行套接件 — Executor 协议（execute / is_ready） TaskHarness 任务套接件 — 任务队列管理（push / get_next / cancel） EventHarness 事件套接件 — 日志和事件发布 StorageHarness 存储套接件 — 进度日志和历史持久化 ContextHarness 上下文套接件 — prompt / context 组装 LifecycleHarness 生命周期套接件 — pre/post task 钩子调用
